report_name: Megalodon Attack: 5,561 GitHub Repos Compromised in Automated CI/CD Onslaught

threat_actors.name: TeamPCP
threat_actors.description: A threat actor group that executed the large-scale, automated 'Megalodon' supply chain attack against 5,561 public GitHub repositories. The group leveraged compromised developer credentials to inject malicious CI/CD workflows for secret exfiltration and deployed wiper malware against targets in Iran and Israel.
threat_actors.goals: The primary objective was the exfiltration of sensitive secrets and credentials from software projects, with a secondary objective of geopolitical disruption through destructive attacks.

attack_patterns.name: Credentials from Password Stores
attack_patterns.description: Used as the likely initial access vector, where the attackers obtained developer GitHub credentials through infostealer malware campaigns targeting developers' machines.

attack_patterns.name: Valid Accounts
attack_patterns.description: Compromised developer GitHub accounts were used to automate the process of pushing 5,718 malicious commits across 5,561 repositories.

attack_patterns.name: Poisoned Pipeline Execution
attack_patterns.description: Attackers modified CI/CD pipeline configuration files within the .github/workflows/ directory to include malicious steps. This acted as a persistence mechanism, executing malicious code to exfiltrate secrets whenever the CI/CD pipeline was triggered.

attack_patterns.name: Transfer Data to Cloud Account
attack_patterns.description: Malicious CI/CD workflows were designed to capture and exfiltrate sensitive data, including API keys, tokens, and other secrets, to an attacker-controlled command-and-control server.

attack_patterns.name: Data Destruction
attack_patterns.description: Wiper malware was deployed against specific targets in Iran and Israel, indicating a dual purpose of causing disruption and damage in addition to data theft.

malware.name: Infostealer
malware.description: A generic class of malware suspected to be the initial access vector for the Megalodon campaign, used to harvest developer credentials from their workstations.

malware.name: Wiper
malware.description: A destructive type of malware deployed by TeamPCP against entities in Iran and Israel to cause damage and disruption as part of the Megalodon campaign.

campaigns.name: Megalodon
campaigns.description: A large-scale, automated supply chain attack conducted by TeamPCP on May 18, 2026. The campaign compromised 5,561 public GitHub repositories by injecting malicious CI/CD workflows to exfiltrate secrets and deploy wiper malware against specific geopolitical targets.

identities.name: Public GitHub Repositories
identities.description: A class of 5,561 public repositories on GitHub that were compromised during the Megalodon campaign. Affected projects included open-source projects, cloud infrastructure tools, developer utilities, and cryptocurrency platforms.

identities.name: Iranian organizations
identities.description: A class of organizations located in Iran that were specifically targeted with destructive wiper malware during the Megalodon campaign.

identities.name: Israeli organizations
identities.description: A class of organizations located in Israel that were specifically targeted with destructive wiper malware during the Megalodon campaign.

infrastructure.name: Megalodon C2 Server
infrastructure.description: A remote command-and-control server used by TeamPCP to receive exfiltrated environment variables, secrets, and other credentials from the malicious CI/CD workflows.

indicators.name: Suspicious CI/CD Environment Variable Enumeration
indicators.pattern:
title: Suspicious CI/CD Environment Variable Enumeration
id: 7e8b1c2a-f3d4-4e5f-8a9b-0c1d2e3f4a5b
status: experimental
description: Detects commands in a CI/CD pipeline attempting to list environment variables containing secrets, as seen in the Megalodon campaign.
references:
  - https://www.securityweek.com/docketwise-data-breach-impacts-143000/
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-05-25
tags:
  - attack.t1552.001
  - attack.collection
level: medium
logsource:
  category: process_creation
detection:
  selection:
    CommandLine|re: 'env\s*\|\s*grep.*(SECRET|TOKEN|KEY|PASSWORD)'
  condition: selection
indicators.description: Detects CI/CD pipeline commands attempting to list environment variables containing secrets like 'SECRET', 'TOKEN', 'KEY', or 'PASSWORD'.

indicators.name: Suspicious CI/CD Data Exfiltration via cURL
indicators.pattern:
title: Suspicious CI/CD Data Exfiltration via cURL
id: 9a8b7c6d-5e4f-4d3c-2b1a-0f9e8d7c6b5a
status: experimental
description: Detects the use of cURL in a CI/CD pipeline to POST data to an external endpoint, a TTP used in the Megalodon campaign for secret exfiltration.
references:
  - https://www.securityweek.com/docketwise-data-breach-impacts-143000/
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-05-25
tags:
  - attack.t1537
  - attack.exfiltration
level: medium
logsource:
  category: process_creation
detection:
  selection:
    Image|endswith: '/curl'
    CommandLine|contains|all:
      - '-X POST'
      - '-d'
  condition: selection
indicators.description: Detects the use of cURL with POST requests in CI/CD logs, a common pattern for exfiltrating captured data to an external server.

indicators.name: Unauthorized GitHub Workflow Modification
indicators.pattern:
title: Unauthorized GitHub Workflow Modification
id: 1a2b3c4d-5e6f-7a8b-9c0d-1e2f3a4b5c6d
status: experimental
description: Detects modification of GitHub Actions workflow files, a technique used in the Megalodon supply chain attack.
references:
  - https://www.securityweek.com/docketwise-data-breach-impacts-143000/
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-05-25
tags:
  - attack.t1677
  - attack.persistence
level: high
logsource:
  category: file_event
detection:
  selection:
    TargetFilename|contains: '.github/workflows/'
    TargetFilename|endswith:
      - '.yml'
      - '.yaml'
  condition: selection
indicators.description: Detects modifications to GitHub Actions workflow files (`.github/workflows/*.yml`), which was the core persistence and execution method in the Megalodon attack.

relationships.source_name: Suspicious CI/CD Data Exfiltration via cURL
